payload.body:
Agent implementation completed and the managed branch was pushed.

I’ll trace the ticket/task data model and completion flow first, then implement persistence, UI rendering, and focused tests. I’ll preserve the existing worktree state and won’t commit or push.The repository already has a durable worktree handover record and a completion hook, but the current hook only posts a free-form comment and the task API does not project the handoff. I’m narrowing the change around that existing boundary so failed pushes can still leave a structured, inspectable partial handoff.I’ve settled on a structured handoff contract rather than relying on comment text: the durable record will carry review state, push status/error, base ref, changed files, test evidence, artifacts, and limitations. The API will project that onto agent tasks, while the ticket sidebar renders it and supplies copy/open actions.The first compile check is blocked only because this prepared worktree has no `node_modules`. I’ll finish the focused tests, install from the lockfile, then run package-level and repository checks.Focused coverage is passing for durable persistence (including failed push state), API projection/merge commands, and the rendered handoff card. I’m now checking migration safety, formatting/lint, and the broader package suites for regressions.One cleanup surfaced in the final diff review: formatting the Git command file touched many pre-existing lines. I’m restoring that file’s original layout and reapplying only the new changed-files command so the handoff stays reviewable.Implementation and focused verification are complete. The remaining full-suite failures are environmental/pre-existing checks: the API tracing assertion never receives its HTTP span, and the desktop suite’s server test cannot bind the already-used local port `4738`; all ticket-specific tests and affected package tests pass.Implemented UKN-E67PU. The handoff is now durable, exposed through agent-task APIs, and rendered on ticket/task detail.

Key changes:

- Added structured merge handoff data: state, branch, push status/error, commits, changed files, tests/results, artifacts, risks/limitations/follow-ups, and merge commands.
- Added additive SQLite migration and persistence for complete and partial/failed handoffs.
- Persisted structured ticket comments for successful and failed handoffs.
- Added `merge_ready`, `needs_user_input`, `failed`, and `abandoned` UI states.
- Added copyable safe `git fetch` / `git switch` / `git merge --ff-only` commands.
- Added GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket branch links when a remote URL is available.
- Removed automatic PR creation from the handoff flow.
- Added exact Git changed-file collection and completion-summary evidence extraction.
- Added the handoff card in [ViewIssuePanel.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/.cycle/worktrees/repo_16371/worktree_mrf8p47f_akaeqoov84wy/packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ViewIssuePanel.tsx) and [MergeHandoffCard.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/.cycle/worktrees/repo_16371/worktree_mrf8p47f_akaeqoov84wy/packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/MergeHandoffCard.tsx).

Verification:

- `pnpm typecheck` — passed
- Changed-file lint and `git diff --check` — passed
- Git tests — 4 passed
- Git-worktrees tests — 7 passed
- Backend tests — 5 passed
- Focused API handoff tests — 3 passed
- Focused desktop rendering tests — 2 passed

Broader-suite notes:

- API suite: 39 passed, 1 unrelated tracing-span assertion failed.
- Desktop suite: 79 passed, 1 server test failed because local port `4738` is already in use.
- Global formatting check reports 455 pre-existing formatting issues across the repository.

No screenshots were generated. No commit or push was performed; the prepared worktree is currently detached, and Cycle retains ownership of commit, push, and ticket transition.

Task: agent_task_611b22d2a8764e21af1a29eec8273870
Worktree: worktree_mrf8p47f_akaeqoov84wy
Handover: worktree_handover_agent_task_611b22d2a8764e21af1a29eec8273870
